title: AirPro Lockers — Renovation/Addition
openingLine: Hi, I'm working on a renovation and addition for Northcrest High and the athletic director is dead set on AirPro lockers. I have a drawing of the locker rooms but I'm not sure how to lay them out or what sizes I should be specifying. Can you help me?
customerContext: You're a competent architect but lockers aren't your specialty. The athletic director already saw AirPro at a tradeshow and wants them — your job is to make it work in the space. You don't want to over-promise or look like you don't know what you're doing.

hiddenInfo.reveal: Two team locker rooms (boys/girls), one coaches' room, and one general PE locker bay.
Football, basketball, wrestling, soccer, track. Football is the biggest user.
Drawings show generic boxes. I'd rather you recommend sizes based on actual sport use.
Athletic director has seen AirPro at a tradeshow. Coaches haven't seen it in person.
$24M renovation/addition. Locker scope is in the $400-500K range.
I'd put Wenger as BOD if you'll support me with spec language. District allows BOD specs.
Storage rooms behind the locker rooms — could use carts or shelving but I haven't spec'd them yet.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Asked which sports use each room
Asked whether locker sizes should match drawings or be recommended
Confirmed whether end users have seen the offering

Solution Framing.behavior: Recommended locker sizes based on fit and sport-specific use
Offered to create drawings and provide specifications
Offered BOD spec language to lock in Wenger

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Asked about carts or shelving in adjacent storage spaces
Inquired about other spaces in the project needing storage solutions

BANT.behavior: Discussed budget considerations for the locker package
Asked when the project will bid

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Mapped Wenger deliverables to the 11-week bid window
Set follow-up tasks with specific dates

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Clearly explained next steps before ending the call
Offered to walk the AD or coaches through the product in person
